SACRAMENTO ADDRESS 
ST A TE CAPITOL 
SACRAMENTO 95814 
Assembly 
, 
O1alifornia 11legislatnre 
BYRON SHER 
ASSEMBL YMAN. TWENTY-FIRST DISTRICT 
Ms. Barbara Milman 
Chief, Legal Division 
March 3, 1981 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
1100 K Street 
Sacramento, Ca 
95814 
Dear Ms. Milman: 
A constituent has requested that I seek your advice re-
garding the possible existence of a conflict of interest with 
regard to a potential elected officeholder in a local govern-
ing body. 
The potential officeholder is a member of an Association 
which engages in the practice of real estate appraisal. 
The 
rights and duties of members of the Association are set out in 
the enclosed Articles of Association and Bill of Sale. 
A member 
of the Association other than the potential officeholder has as 
a client the entity whose governing body the potential office-
holder would like to join. 
As I understand it, there is neither 
fee splitting nor profit-sharing among members of the Association. 
In particular, the constituent is concerned whether: 
1. 
the potential officeholder would have to disqualify 
him or her self with regard to any matters concerlLing clients 
of other members of the Association; and 
2. 
any general conflict considerations arise by virtue 
of the relationship between the entity and another member 
of the Association. 
I would appreciate receiving an Advice Letter from you regard-
ing these questions, and any other considerations you deem relevant. 
Thank you very much. 
Enclosure 
BDS:wlc 
Sincerely, 
'1.r~ 
Byron D. Sher 
Assemblyman 
21st District 


ARTICLES OF ASSOCIATION 
If] 
voluntarily associate themselves as Associates pursuant to the terms and 
conditions set forth in these Articles of Association. 
ARTICLE I. 
NATURE OF ASSOCIATION 
Type of Business 
1.01 
The Associates shal I individually engage in the practice of 
real estate appraisal and the purpose of the Association shal I be for the 
pooling of resources and for the sharing of expenses incurred in the business 
of real estate appraisal. 
Name of Association 
Term of Associa ion 
1.03 
The Association shall commence on January I, 19 
dnd sha I I 
continue until terminated under the terms of this Agreement. 
ARTICLE 2. 
FINANCIAL 
Initial Capital 
2.01 
Each individual Associate shal I contribute to the Association 
his interest in case files, statistical information, furniture, fixtures, 
and equipment on the premises at the principal office of the Assocation or 
in storage at another location. 
Each Associate wi II be considered to have 
contributed an amount of a value of Twelve Thousand Five Hundred (SI2,500) 
liars. 


from each Associate to be used to pay for the monthly expenses. 
Each 
Associate wi I I be a signatory for the account and each check drawn upon 
the association account wil I require the signature of at least (2) 
Associates. 
Each Associate, in turn, shall act as the business manager 
and handle the detai I, office and general work of running the Association 
and its facil ities for a six (6) month period. 
The first managing Associate

2.06 
Each Associate shal I have an equal voice In any decision to 
expend Association funds. 
Any decision as to the expenditure of funds 
not in the ordinary course of business shall require 3 majority vote of 
the Associates, each Associate having one (I) vote. 
2.0J 
No Associate shal I sel I, assign, morts~ge, hypothecate or 
encumber his interest in the Association. 
ARTICLE 3. 
TERMINATION OF ASSOCIATION 
Withdrawal of an Associate 
3.01 
Any Associate may voluntari Iy withdraw from the Association 
by giving al I other Associates at least sixty (60) days notice of his inten-
tion to do so. 
Option to Purchase Terminated Interest 
3.02 
On dissolution of the Association by reason of the death of 
an Associate or voluntary withdrawal of an Associate, the remaining ~ssociates 
shall have an option to purchase the interest of the deceased or withdraw-ing 
Associate In the assets and goodwi I I of the Association, 
paying to such 
Associate or the person legally entitled 
thereto, the value of such 
jnter~st 
determined as provided in Paragraph 3.03 of these Articl s. 


Withdrawal of Ca i 
2.02 No portion of the ini tial contribution or subsequent contribu-
tions to the Association may be wi thdrawn by any Associate at any time 
without the express written consent of al I Associates. 
Books of Account 
2.03 
Complete and accurate accounts of all transactions of the 
Association shal I be kept in proper books, and each of the Associates 
shal I enter. or cause to be entered therein, a ful I and accurate account 
of al I his transactions on behal f of the Association. 
Inspection of Books 
2.04 
The books of account and other records of the Association 
shall. at all times, be kept in the principal place of business of the 
Association and each of the Associates shal I, at al I times. have access to, 
and may inspect and copy any of them. 
2.05 
As stated above, the primary purpose of the Association is 
for the sharing of expenses incurred in the operation of a real estate 
appraisal practice. 
Therefore. it is agreed among the Associates that each 
Associate shal I bear an equal portion of the operating expenses on a monthly 
basis. 
Each month, each Associate shal I be bi I led for a one-quarter share 
of the expenses incurred for the prior month. 
Each month each Associate 
shal I cause to be deposited into the Association bank account his share of the 
operating expenses. 
2.06 
The Association shal I establ ish a separate Association bank 
account in the name of the Association at such bank or banks in Santa Clara 
County, Ca Ii 
rnia as may from time to time be selected by a majority vote of 
al I the Associates. 
The purpose of this account wi I I be to accept deposits

within sixty (60) days after the appointr.ent and qual ification 
of such personal representative. 
b. 
If the Association is dissolved by the voluntary withdrawal of 
an Associate, by serving written notice of the exercise of such 
option on the withdrawing Associate within sixty (60) days after

Purchase Price of Partnership Interest 
3.03 
On exercise of the option described in Paragraph 3.02 of these 
Articles to purchase the Association interest of a deceased or withdrawing 
Associate, the remaining Associates shal I pay to the person legally entitled 
thereto, the value of such interest determined as follm'/s: 
Twelve Thousand 
Five Hundred (SI2,500) Dollars plus the actual cost less a reduction of ten 
(100) percent as depreciation for each year of any association purchases sub-
sequen t to Janua ry I, 19 
The aforementioned purchase price wi I I be payable to 
the deceased Associate's personal representative or the withdrawing Associate on 
a term payment over a period of one (I) year at 
10~ interest.

sections.question: In particular, the constituent is concerned whether: 1. the potential officeholder would have to disqualify him or her self with regard to any matters concerning clients of other members of the Association; and 2. any general conflict considerations arise by virtue of the relationship between the entity and another member of the Association.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a potential local officeholder who is a member of a real estate appraisal association would face conflict of interest issues, specifically regarding disqualification on matters involving other association members' clients and general conflict considerations when another association member represents the governing body the officeholder seeks to join.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is heavily redacted/corrupted with OCR errors making portions unreadable. This appears to be an incoming request letter TO the FPPC (from Assemblyman Sher requesting advice), not an FPPC response letter. The document includes attachments (Articles of Association and Bill of Sale) but no FPPC conclusion/advice is present in this extract. Marked as advice_letter because is_fppc_response field requires classification, but this is technically an incoming request for an advice letter. The FPPC's actual response is not included in this document.

embedding.summary: Assemblyman Byron Sher seeks an FPPC advice letter regarding potential conflict of interest issues for a constituent who is a prospective elected official and member of a real estate appraisal association, where another association member represents the entity whose governing body the prospective officeholder wishes to join.
